elements.sent-2411-001: The $B_c$ meson, a unique bound state comprising of two open heavy flavors, charm and bottom, offers a rich avenue for probing the predictions of the Next Decade - Standard Model (ND-SM) physics properties due to its heavy mass.
elements.sent-2411-002: With recent observations of its excited states, interest in understanding $B_c$ production mechanisms and decay modes has surged.
elements.sent-2411-003: This article presents the current state of art on $B_c$ mesons, encompassing production mechanisms, properties of different decay modes, and theoretical modeling.
elements.sent-2411-004: We present novel findings on the newly constructed ratios $(\mathcal{R _{\eta_c/J/\psi}$, $\mathcal{R _{D/D^* )$ in semileptonic and ($\mathcal{R _\mu^\tau)^{B_c}$ , ($\mathcal{R _\mu^\tau)^{B_c^*}$ in leptonic decays, respectively.}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-005: These results emphasize the importance of $B_c$ studies in the future collider experiments.
elements.sent-2411-006: The article further explores CP effects in $B_c$ meson decays refining our understanding of heavy flavor properties.
elements.sent-2411-007: Finally, potential avenues for future research, and leveraging upcoming collider experiments are outlined.
elements.sent-2411-008: The $B_c$ meson, typically denoting the fundamental $1^1 S_0$ state of $(c \bar b)$-quarkonium with a combination of two distinct heavy flavors, presents a distinct bound-state arrangement ideal for scrutinizing the predictions of the Standard Model (SM).
elements.sent-2411-009: This feature has generated considerable attention from researchers because of its heavy mass.
elements.sent-2411-010: Ever since the discovery of $B_c$ meson in Fermilab by the Collider Detector (CDF) Collaborations \cite{CDF:1998ihx in 1998, the experimental probe to detect its family members in their ground and excited states continues over last two decades.}
elements.sent-2411-011: With the observation of around 20 occurrences of $B_c$ events in the $B_c \to J/\psi l \nu_l$ decay mode, highlights the potential for experimental investigation of $B_c$ meson at hadron colliders\cite{Wester:2005ex,Cheung:1999ir .}
elements.sent-2411-012: A comprehensive investigation of the $B_c$ family members is anticipated at the Large Hadron Collider (LHC), where the increased energy and significantly higher luminosity will enable more detailed studies.
elements.sent-2411-013: The lifetime of $B_c$ has been measured \cite{D0:2008bqs,D0:2008thm,CDF:2006kbk,CDF:2007umr using decay channels: ${B_c ^{\pm \rightarrow J/\psi e^{\pm \nu_e$ and ${B_c ^{\pm \rightarrow J/\psi \pi^ {\pm}$.}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-014: A more precise measurement of $B_c$ lifetime: $\tau_{B_c =0.51^{+0.18 _{-0.16 (stat.)\pm 0.03(syst.)$ ps and its mass: $M =6.40\pm 0.39\pm 0.13\; \text{GeV}$ have been obtained \cite{LHCb:2014ilr using the decay mode $B_c \rightarrow J/\psi \mu \nu_\mu X$, where $X$ denotes any possible additional particle in the final state.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-015: Research into the production of the $B_c$ meson holds significance for understanding this particle, as it elucidates the quantity of $B_c$ events feasible in a collider setting.
elements.sent-2411-016: Conversely, once the production rate is determined, theoretical models regarding $B_c$ production can be validated through the measurement of its cascade decays.
elements.sent-2411-017: Indeed, their production yields are significantly smaller than those of the charmonium and bottomonium states.
elements.sent-2411-018: The production process for the $\bar b c$ system differs significantly from that of the $\bar b b$ system, primarily due to the necessity of creating two heavy quark-antiquark pairs during a collision.
elements.sent-2411-019: While the $\bar b b$ pair can be generated through parton processes like $\bar q q$, $gg \to b\bar b$ at the level of $\alpha_s^2$, the fundamental mechanism for creating the $\bar b c$ system is considerably more complex, at least proportional to the fourth power of the strong coupling constant, $\alpha_S^4$: $q\bar q$ $gg \to (\bar b c) b \bar c$.
elements.sent-2411-020: At energies typical of the Tevatron and LHC, the gluon-gluon contribution dominates since the production of two pairs of heavy quarks is required.
elements.sent-2411-021: With an LHC luminosity of approximately $\mathcal{L = 10^{34 cm^{-2 s^{-1}$, an estimated yield of around $5 \times 10^{10}$ $B_c$ events per year could be anticipated \cite{Gouz:2002kk .}}}}
elements.sent-2411-022: At present, most studies of the $B_c$ production focus on the hadronic production due to the high production rate at a hadron collider, such as Tevatron or LHC \cite{Chang:1992jb,Chang:2004bh,Chang:1992pt,Chang:2005wd,Cheung:1999ir,Chen:2018obq .}
elements.sent-2411-023: Indeed, the $B_c$ meson has been only observed at hadron colliders up to now \cite{CDF:1998axz,CDF:1998ihx,CDF:2012ksy,LHCb:2012ihf,LHCb:2014ilr,LHCb:2017lpu,LHCb:2019bem,CMS:2019uhm .}
elements.sent-2411-024: In addition to hadron colliders, a high luminosity $e^+$ $e^-$ collider can be a potentially good platform for studying the $B_c$ meson.
elements.sent-2411-025: Compared with the hadron collider, there are less backgrounds at the $e^+$ $e^-$ collider, thus it is suitable for studying the $B_c$ meson precisely.
elements.sent-2411-026: Moreover, the production mechanism of the $B_c$ meson at the $e^+$ $e^-$ collider is much simpler than that at the hadron collider.
elements.sent-2411-027: The $B_c$ mesons cannot be produced at the B-factories, which operate at the $\Upsilon(5S)$ peak.
elements.sent-2411-028: The reason is that the $\Upsilon$ has sufficient mass to produce up to a pair of $B_s$ but not a pair of $B_c$ mesons.
elements.sent-2411-029: As a result, the production of $B_c$ mesons is exclusive to the LHC.
elements.sent-2411-030: Recent research on the production of $B_c$ mesons at $e^+e^-$ colliders is detailed in Refs~ \cite{Chen:2020dtu,Zheng:2018fqv .}
elements.sent-2411-031: The $B_c$ meson provides a specific possibility for investigating the production and decay mechanisms of heavy mesons.
elements.sent-2411-032: The reason is two fold: first, it is only the $B_c$ that contains two heavy quarks and at the same time carries explicit flavor numbers since for the top quark there is not enough time to form hadrons before decaying weakly due to the large top quark mass \cite{D0:1995jca , secondly, because of the absence of decays caused by strong and electromagnetic interactions, the lightest pseudoscalar state $B_c$ can only decay weakly and is therefore long lived.}
elements.sent-2411-033: Moreover, the $B_c$ meson serves as a favorable context for determining the Cabibbo-Kobayashi-Maskawa (CKM) matrix elements $V_{cb}$ and $V_{ub}$.
elements.sent-2411-034: Precise calculations of hadronic parameters are crucial for extracting CKM matrix elements by comparing SM predictions with experimental data on exclusive flavor-changing decay rates.
elements.sent-2411-035: Like heavy quarkonia such as $\eta_c$, $J/\psi$, $\eta_b$, and $\Upsilon$, among others, the production cross section of the $B_c$ meson can also be studied down using factorization theories such as non-relativistic QCD (NRQCD) \cite{Bodwin:1994jh .}
elements.sent-2411-036: This approach enables the calculation of significant details regarding the production of $B_c$ through perturbative methods.
elements.sent-2411-037: Heavy quarkonia mesons, composed primarily of heavy (i.e., $c$ or $b$) quark pairs in a valence approximation, serve as a unique laboratory for studying strong interactions across both perturbative and non-perturbative domains.
elements.sent-2411-038: On a partonic level, the processes involving their production and decay can be elucidated in terms of the creation or annihilation of heavy quarks, which subsequently hadronize into the observed particles.
elements.sent-2411-039: Doubly heavy mesons with an open flavor occupy an intermediate position between charmonium and bottomonium particles, allowing them to be utilized as a testing ground for models devised to characterize heavy quarkonia particles with hidden flavor.
elements.sent-2411-040: In contrast to quarkonium production, where the color-octet contribution typically holds significance \cite{Brambilla:2010cs, Andronic:2015wma, Lansberg:2019adr the production mechanism of the $B_c$ meson is more straightforward.}
elements.sent-2411-041: This is because the color-octet contribution is consistently suppressed in $B_c$ production processes compared to the color-singlet contribution.
elements.sent-2411-042: The observations of the 2S excited states, $B_c(2^{1 S_0)$ and $B_c^{* (2^{3 S_1)$, have been reported by the CMS and LHCb collaborations\cite{CMS:2019uhm,LHCb:2019bem .}}}}
elements.sent-2411-043: As the $B_c$ meson encompasses two distinct heavy flavors, its excited states below the $B \bar D$ threshold typically undergo direct or indirect decay to the ground state through electromagnetic or strong interactions with nearly 100\% probability.
elements.sent-2411-044: These excited states serve as significant sources of the $B_c$ meson.
elements.sent-2411-045: Thus, the study on the production of the two excited states is inherently intriguing.
elements.sent-2411-046: In the upcoming sections, we will analyze the various decay processes of the $B_c$ meson and highlight new findings on newly constructed ratios in semileptonic and leptonic decays in view of the development of advanced energetic colliders, probing more understanding in the Unexplored Physics (UP) sectors.

elements.sent-2411-131: Accurately computing these effects would greatly improve our ability to identify the source of CP violation in weak interactions, using the extensive data on these decays being gathered at the B factories.
elements.sent-2411-132: The two-body non-leptonic $B_c$ decays have been widely studied using various theoretical approaches and phenomenological models.
elements.sent-2411-133: The effective Hamiltonian describing the $B_c$ non-leptonic decays into meson states ($M_1, M_2$) is given by: \begin{equation}\mathcal{A}(B_c \to M_1 M_2) = \frac{G_F}{\sqrt{2}} \lambda_c \sum_{i} {\cal C}_i(\mu) \langle M_1M_2|{\cal O}_i(\mu)|B_c\rangle_i\end{equation} \begin{equation}{\cal} H_{eff} = \frac{G_F}{\sqrt2} |V_{cb}| |V_{cq}|^\dag \sum_{i=1}^{6} {\cal} C_i {\cal} O_i\end{equation} In exclusive non-leptonic modes usually involves the approximation of factorization \cite{Buras:1994ij which, as expected, can be quite accurate for the $B_c$, since the quark-gluon sea is suppressed in the heavy quarkonium..}
elements.sent-2411-134: In the factorization hypothesis, non-leptonic meson decays can be categorized into three classes: class I, involving only color-favored tree diagrams (external W-emission diagrams) with a decay amplitude proportional to the QCD factor $a_1$; class II, involving only color-suppressed tree diagrams (internal W- emission) with a decay amplitude proportional to $a_2$; and class III, involving both external and internal W-emission contributions.
elements.sent-2411-135: Thus, the important parameters are the factors $a_1$ and $a_2$ in the non-leptonic weak Lagrangian, which depend on the normalization point suitable for the $B_c$ decays.
elements.sent-2411-136: The comprehensive predictions for non-leptonic decay modes of the $B_c$ meson within the RIQM framework are presented in~\cite{Dash:2023hjr .}
elements.sent-2411-137: The CPV in the $B_c$ decays can be investigated in the same manner as made in the B decays.
elements.sent-2411-138: However, comparatively little focus has been directed towards studying these phenomena in $B_c$ meson decays \cite{Masetti:1992in .}
elements.sent-2411-139: Therefore it is particularly intriguing to explore the CPV effects in $B_c$ mesons using upcoming colliders.
elements.sent-2411-140: In $B_c$ decays, various diagram types may contribute, including the color-favored tree diagram (external W emission diagram), the color-suppressed tree diagram (internal W emission diagram), the tree-annihilation diagram (W-annihilation diagram), the spacelike penguin diagram, and the timelike penguin diagram.
elements.sent-2411-141: Electroweak penguin diagrams contributions are minor compared to QCD penguin diagrams.
elements.sent-2411-142: In $B_c$ decays, CPV effects arise from contributions by color-favored tree diagrams, color-suppressed tree diagrams, and QCD penguin diagrams.
elements.sent-2411-143: Among these, tree diagram contributions are the most significant.
elements.sent-2411-144: The interference between tree diagrams and QCD penguin diagrams plays a much more critical role than the interference between the penguin diagrams themselves \cite{Liu:1997hr .}
elements.sent-2411-145: In $B_c$ decays, only direct CP violation is possible, necessitating at least two contributing diagrams with different weak phases from the CKM matrix \cite{Cabibbo:1963yz and distinct strong phases from final state interactions.}
elements.sent-2411-146: However, the direct study of CP-violation in the $B_c$ decays is practically difficult because of low relative yield of $B_c$ with respect to ordinary B mesons: $\sigma_{B_c /\sigma_{B \sim 10^{-3}$.}}
elements.sent-2411-147: With QCD penguin diagrams contributing, hard strong phases can be roughly estimated perturbatively, and CP violation arises from interference among tree diagrams, QCD penguin diagrams, and within QCD penguin diagrams themselves.
elements.sent-2411-148: However, this estimation does not encompass potential soft strong phases from final state interaction.
elements.sent-2411-149: When one neglects soft strong phases, CPV in $B_c$ decays is restricted to processes with at least QCD penguin diagrams contributing.
elements.sent-2411-150: From an experimental perspective, the best $B_c$ decay modes that are promising for testing CP violation include $B_c \to J\psi D^*$ and $B_c \to \eta_c D^{(*)}$.
